title: brickellia cuspidata
intro: Brickellia cuspidata, also known as Mountain Sage or Cuspidate Brickellia, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Brickellia cuspidata include reducing fever, treating colds and flu, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it a popular choice among herbalists for its potential therapeutic benefits.
